changelog.summary: Initial seven-phase consumer-side self-update integrity playbook. Covers the receiving end of the software supply chain that publisher-side posture (library-author, supply-chain-integrity) does not: a self-update that applies without verifying a signature, verifies against an in-band (attacker-suppliable) key, accepts a signed-but-older version (downgrade / no anti-rollback), fetches over an unauthenticated channel, serves browser modules without Subresource Integrity, ignores C2PA content credentials or SCITT/TSA transparency receipts on received artifacts, or applies the update without gating on the verifier result. Maps to ATT&CK T1195.002 / T1574 and to NIST 800-53 SR-11 / SI-7, ISO 27001 A.8.19, NIS2 Art.21, and EU CRA Annex I secure-update obligations.

preconditions.description: Agent must read the operator's self-update / artifact-consumption source and/or runtime configuration to inspect signature verification, key pinning, anti-rollback, channel pinning, SRI, and provenance/transparency checks. A host with neither marks the playbook visibility_gap=no_update_path_inventory.

theater_fingerprints.claim: Our updates are signed, so the update channel is secure.
theater_fingerprints.fast_detection_test: Signing is the PUBLISHER side. Ask whether the CLIENT verifies the signature, against a key pinned OUT OF BAND, BEFORE applying — and refuses downgrades. A signed update the client does not verify is theater.

theater_fingerprints.claim: Updates come over HTTPS, so they cannot be tampered.
theater_fingerprints.fast_detection_test: HTTPS authenticates against a CA bundle and is defeated by a mis-issued cert. Ask whether the artifact itself is signature-verified with a pinned key independent of the channel.
Ask whether the APPLY step is GATED on the verifier result and fails closed. A verifier whose output does not block activation is decorative.

framework_context.gap_summary: Org supply-chain controls focus on the publisher: signing, SBOM, SLSA. They do not require the CONSUMER side — that the receiving client verifies signatures against a pinned key before applying, refuses downgrades, pins the channel, enforces SRI on browser modules, and verifies provenance/transparency receipts.

per_framework_gaps.insufficient_because: attested by "updates are signed" without verifying the client checks the signature against a pinned key before applying and refuses downgrades.

per_framework_gaps.designed_for: secure updates for products with digital elements
per_framework_gaps.insufficient_because: conformance is attested by shipping signed updates without verifying the receiving client enforces signature + anti-rollback + key-pin.

direct.threat_context: The self-update loop is the highest-privilege code path most products ship: it fetches code and runs it as the application. Publisher-side signing (library-author, supply-chain-integrity) is necessary but useless if the CONSUMER applies updates without verifying the signature, trusts a key the channel supplies, accepts a signed-but-vulnerable older version, or applies before the verifier gates it. Browser-served modules add the Subresource-Integrity surface. These are consumer-side validation gaps, not publisher posture.
direct.framework_lag_declaration: A clean "updates are signed / SLSA-attested / SBOM-published" audit is NON-EVIDENCE for consumer-side update integrity; it confirms publisher posture, not signature-before-apply, key-pinning, anti-rollback, or verifier-gating on the receiving client.

skill_chain.purpose: enumerate the consumer-side verify / pin / anti-rollback / SRI / provenance checks
cross-reference the publisher-side signing/SLSA posture this consumes
assess the update-signature algorithm for downgrade / non-PQC exposure
map findings to the SR-11 / SI-7 / CRA gaps the controls do not cover

artifacts.source: grep for self-update / self-update-standalone-verifier / downloadUpdate / applyUpdate / signature / verify across the update subsystem and config.
artifacts.description: How the self-update fetches, verifies, and applies releases, and whether the apply is gated on the verifier.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the self-update source for the fetch/verify/apply sequence and the signature + verifier-gate logic.

artifacts.source: grep for public key / root / pin / TOFU / https / cert / version / rollback / minimum-version across the update trust + channel config.
artifacts.description: Whether the verifying key is pinned out-of-band, the channel is pinned, and anti-rollback / monotonic-version is enforced.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the updater for key-pin source, channel pinning, and the version-comparison logic.

artifacts.source: grep for importmap-integrity / import map / integrity= / subresource / module script across the browser-served asset pipeline.
artifacts.description: Whether browser-served modules / import maps carry Subresource Integrity hashes.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the asset pipeline / template for integrity= on module scripts and the importmap.

artifacts.source: grep for content-credentials / c2pa / scitt / tsa / transparency / receipt / timestamp across the artifact-consumption path.
artifacts.description: Whether C2PA content credentials and SCITT/TSA transparency receipts on received artifacts are verified.
artifacts.air_gap_alternative: Inspect the artifact-consumption path for manifest + receipt verification where provenance is relied upon.

collection_scope.time_window: current update-path configuration + source state (point-in-time posture audit)
collection_scope.asset_scope: every self-updating client/agent and every consumer of externally-sourced artifacts (modules, models, signed bundles)

environment_assumptions.assumption: The product has a self-update path or consumes externally-sourced executable artifacts.
environment_assumptions.if_false: A product updated solely via an OS package manager that verifies signatures has a reduced surface; focus on browser-module SRI and provenance if applicable.

environment_assumptions.assumption: Update-path source or config is readable.
environment_assumptions.if_false: Mark visibility_gap=no_update_path_inventory and report only what the fetch/verify/apply source reveals.

fallback_if_unavailable.fallback_action: If the product serves no browser modules, skip the SRI indicator.
If no C2PA / SCITT / TSA is used, skip those indicators.

indicators.value: The self-update path fetches and applies a new release without verifying a cryptographic signature over the downloaded artifact before swapping it in.
indicators.description: An attacker who can serve or tamper with the update (compromised mirror, MITM, poisoned CDN) delivers arbitrary code that the client installs as a trusted update — the canonical software-supply-chain foothold.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the update path verifies a signature over the artifact BEFORE applying it (not merely a checksum the same server provides) — a path that applies on download, or trusts a server-provided hash with no signature, is the finding.
A self-update that delegates to an OS package manager which itself verifies signatures (apt/rpm/notary) is safe; the finding is a bespoke self-update with no signature step.

indicators.value: The self-update accepts a correctly-signed but OLDER version (no monotonic version / anti-rollback check), so a downgrade attack is possible.
indicators.description: A downgrade serves a genuinely-signed but known-vulnerable prior release; without anti-rollback the client willingly reinstalls a version with a patched CVE, re-opening it.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the updater refuses a target version lower than the installed version (monotonic counter / minimum-version floor) — accepting any signed version regardless of order is the finding.
A deliberate, operator-initiated rollback path (explicit downgrade command) is not this finding; the finding is the AUTOMATIC update accepting an older signed version.

indicators.value: The update signature is verified against a key/root that the update channel itself can supply or rotate without an out-of-band pin (TOFU with no pinned root, or a key fetched from the same endpoint as the update).
indicators.description: If the signing key is not pinned out-of-band, an attacker who controls the channel supplies their own key + signature and the verification passes — signature verification with an attacker-chosen key is no verification.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the verifying root key is pinned (shipped in the binary / OS trust store / a separate pinned channel) and not fetched from the same place as the update — a key delivered alongside the update is the finding.
A documented, audited key-rotation that re-pins out-of-band is safe; the finding is in-band key trust.

indicators.value: The update is fetched over plaintext HTTP, or over HTTPS without certificate/SPKI pinning where the channel is the sole integrity control.
indicators.description: A plaintext or unpinned channel lets an on-path attacker substitute the update; even with HTTPS, a mis-issued cert defeats CA-only trust for the update endpoint.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the update is fetched over TLS with the endpoint pinned (or that artifact-signature verification makes the channel non-load-bearing) — plaintext fetch, or HTTPS-only as the sole control, is the finding.
If the artifact is independently signature-verified with a pinned key, the channel is not the sole control and a weaker channel is acceptable.

indicators.value: Browser-served module scripts / import maps are delivered without Subresource Integrity (no integrity= hashes), so a tampered or substituted module loads without detection.
indicators.description: Without SRI, a compromised CDN or MITM swaps a JavaScript module the page imports; the browser executes it with the page's full privileges — a client-side supply-chain compromise.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm import-map entries / module script tags carry integrity= hashes matching the served bytes (and the importmap itself is integrity-protected) — modules served without SRI are the finding.
First-party modules served same-origin over a pinned channel have a reduced need for SRI; the finding is third-party / CDN-served modules without integrity hashes.

indicators.value: C2PA / content-credential (provenance manifest) bound to a received artifact (model, media, signed bundle) is present but not verified, or is absent where the consumer relies on provenance.
indicators.description: Unverified content credentials let a tampered or mis-attributed artifact pass as provenance-checked; the manifest exists but proves nothing if the consumer never validates the claim chain to a trust anchor.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the consumer verifies the C2PA manifest signature + claim chain to a trusted signer where it relies on provenance — accepting the manifest on its face (or ignoring it) is the finding.
A consumer that does not rely on content provenance for any trust decision is not in scope for this indicator.

indicators.value: Received artifacts carry SCITT transparency-log receipts or TSA timestamps that the consumer does not verify (existence + inclusion proof + timestamp validity).
indicators.description: An unverified transparency receipt provides no non-repudiation or timestamp guarantee; an attacker omits or forges it and the consumer accepts an unlogged artifact as transparently-recorded.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the consumer verifies the SCITT receipt inclusion proof / TSA timestamp signature against the expected log/authority — accepting an artifact without checking the receipt is the finding.
A supply chain that does not use transparency logs / timestamping has no receipt to verify and is not in scope.

indicators.value: The standalone update verifier exists but the apply step does not GATE on it — the new binary/code is swapped into the execution path before (or regardless of) the verifier's result.
indicators.description: A verifier whose result does not block the apply is decorative; the attacker-supplied update executes because the swap happened independent of (or before) verification.
false_positive_checks_required: Confirm the apply/swap is conditional on the verifier returning success and fails closed on a verifier error — an apply that proceeds on verifier failure/timeout, or before verification, is the finding.
A two-phase stage-then-verify-then-activate flow where activation is gated on the verifier is safe; the finding is verify-and-apply racing or verifier advisory-only.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the update path verifies a signature over the artifact BEFORE applying it (not merely a checksum the same server provides) — a path that applies on download, or trusts a server-provided hash with no signature, is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the updater refuses a target version lower than the installed version (monotonic counter / minimum-version floor) — accepting any signed version regardless of order is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the verifying root key is pinned (shipped in the binary / OS trust store / a separate pinned channel) and not fetched from the same place as the update — a key delivered alongside the update is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the update is fetched over TLS with the endpoint pinned (or that artifact-signature verification makes the channel non-load-bearing) — plaintext fetch, or HTTPS-only as the sole control, is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm import-map entries / module script tags carry integrity= hashes matching the served bytes (and the importmap itself is integrity-protected) — modules served without SRI are the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the consumer verifies the C2PA manifest signature + claim chain to a trusted signer where it relies on provenance — accepting the manifest on its face (or ignoring it) is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the consumer verifies the SCITT receipt inclusion proof / TSA timestamp signature against the expected log/authority — accepting an artifact without checking the receipt is the finding.

false_positive_profile.benign_pattern: Integrity enforced by a delegated mechanism (OS package manager, pinned-key signature, gated verifier) or a surface where the control is genuinely not relied upon.
false_positive_profile.distinguishing_test: Confirm the apply/swap is conditional on the verifier returning success and fails closed on a verifier error — an apply that proceeds on verifier failure/timeout, or before verification, is the finding.

minimum_signal.detected: At least one consumer-side control is absent on a production update path — no signature verification, in-band key trust, no anti-rollback, unauthenticated channel as sole control, missing SRI, or an apply not gated on the verifier.
minimum_signal.inconclusive: A control is delegated to infrastructure the audit could not read (OS package manager, an external verifier service) — record as visibility_gap, not a clean result.
minimum_signal.not_detected: The update path verifies a signature against an out-of-band-pinned key before applying, refuses downgrades, pins/secures the channel, gates the apply on the verifier, enforces SRI on browser modules, and verifies provenance/transparency receipts where relied upon.

blast_radius_model.scope_question: Does the gap let an attacker who can influence the update channel run arbitrary code on the entire installed base?

scoring_rubric.condition: A self-update with no signature-before-apply on a widely-distributed client — channel compromise yields RCE across the install base
scoring_rubric.description: Mass arbitrary-code execution via the update mechanism

scoring_rubric.condition: Downgrade or in-band-key gap requiring a more specific channel position
scoring_rubric.description: Targeted reintroduction of vulnerable code or key substitution

scoring_rubric.condition: Browser-module SRI gap on a single app surface
scoring_rubric.description: Client-side code substitution scoped to the served page

compliance_theater_check.claim: Our updates are signed and delivered over HTTPS, so the update channel is secure.
compliance_theater_check.audit_evidence: A code-signing certificate, an HTTPS update endpoint, a published SBOM/SLSA attestation.
compliance_theater_check.reality_test: Verify the CLIENT checks the signature against an out-of-band-pinned key before applying, refuses older versions, and gates the apply on the verifier. If a swapped artifact, an attacker-supplied key, or an older signed version would be applied, the signing did not protect the consumer.

framework_gap_mapping.actual_gap: SI-7 is attested by "updates are signed" without verifying the client checks the signature before applying.
CRA conformance via signed updates does not verify the client refuses a signed-but-older vulnerable version.

escalation_criteria.condition: A widely-distributed self-updating client applies updates with no signature-before-apply or in-band key trust
The apply step proceeds on verifier failure/timeout

remediation_paths.description: Verify an artifact signature against an out-of-band-pinned root key BEFORE swapping the update in, and fail closed on any verification error — never trust a server-provided hash alone.
Refuse any target version lower than the installed version (monotonic counter / minimum-version floor); require an explicit operator action for any intentional downgrade.
Fetch over TLS with the endpoint pinned (cert/SPKI), treating the channel as defence-in-depth behind artifact-signature verification.
Add Subresource Integrity hashes to import-map entries and module script tags (and integrity-protect the import map itself) for third-party / CDN-served modules.
Verify C2PA content-credential claim chains and SCITT/TSA transparency receipts on received artifacts where provenance is relied upon.

validation_tests.test: Serve a tampered (or attacker-signed) update artifact to the client.
validation_tests.expected_result: Client rejects: signature does not verify against the pinned key; nothing is applied.

validation_tests.test: Serve a correctly-signed but older version to the auto-updater.
validation_tests.expected_result: Client refuses the downgrade (version below installed).

validation_tests.test: Force the update verifier to fail/timeout during an apply.
validation_tests.expected_result: Apply is blocked (fails closed); the running version is unchanged.

validation_tests.test: Serve a valid, correctly-signed, newer update.
validation_tests.expected_result: Update verifies and applies — no regression on the legitimate path.

residual_risk_statement.risk: Compromise of the pinned signing key (or the publisher's build pipeline) still yields a validly-signed malicious update the consumer will accept.
residual_risk_statement.why_remains: Consumer-side verification authenticates the publisher key, not the publisher's own integrity; build-pipeline / key compromise is the publisher-side supply-chain-integrity concern.

evidence_requirements.description: The signature-verification, key-pin, anti-rollback, channel-pin, SRI, and provenance/transparency configuration as audited.
Outcomes of the tampered-update / downgrade / verifier-failure negative tests plus the legitimate-update functional test.

regression_trigger.condition: A change to the update client, signing key, channel, or artifact-consumption path
Periodic re-attestation of consumer-side update integrity

lesson_template.attack_vector: Malicious update accepted by a consumer that did not verify the signature against a pinned key before applying, refused downgrades, or gated the apply on the verifier — channel compromise to arbitrary code execution.
lesson_template.control_gap: Consumer-side update path lacks signature-before-apply / out-of-band key pin / anti-rollback / verifier-gating.
lesson_template.framework_gap: NIST SR-11 + SI-7 + EU CRA Annex I cover publisher signing but not consumer-side verification enforcement.
lesson_template.new_control_requirement: The update client MUST verify a signature against an out-of-band-pinned key before applying, refuse downgrades, and gate the apply on the verifier.

exception_generation.trigger_condition: A legacy client cannot adopt signature-before-apply immediately (e.g. a third-party updater).

compensating_controls: restrict update sources to a controlled mirror
monitor + alert on update-artifact hash changes

regression_schedule.next_run: quarterly or on any update-client / signing-key / channel change
regression_schedule.trigger: change to the update path, or quarterly re-attestation

directives.title: Inventory + integrity-test every self-update and artifact-consumption path
Targeted anti-rollback + key-pinning sweep on auto-updating clients
